commentaries.author: Aquinas Study Bible
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Now I have come to make you understand what shall befall your people in the latter days: for the vision is for many days yet to come. We need to keep this point in mind as we read these last chapters. They do speak in part of the fate of Israel in the relatively near future. But they refer even more to the last period. The only specific that will be given will refer to Antiochus IV, but yet as we said, it extends even to the end.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Days. It will not soon take place; or, I have many things to tell.

commentaries.author: Jerome
commentaries.content: The very petition which Daniel had requested is the thing which he deserves to hear from God, namely what is going to happen to the people of Israel, not in the near future, but (700) in the last days, that is, at the end of the world.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
